claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit); Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 11 weeks (within 12-week limit)
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to know if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex, he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to determine your eligibility:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! And the next question:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another immigration status?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! One final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

- ✓ Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
- ✓ You live in the UK
- ✓ You are a UK citizen
- ✓ Alex lives with you (you are responsible for him)
- ✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. You can now proceed to make a claim if you haven't already done so.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, though you can still claim the benefit.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and is a UK citizen, satisfying the residency and immigration status requirements. Alex lives with the claimant, which means the claimant is responsible for the child. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so there is no conflict with the rule that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the key requirements: they live in the UK, are a UK citizen, Alex is under 16 and lives with them, they are responsible for Alex, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and is a UK citizen, satisfying the residency and immigration status requirements. Alex lives with the claimant, which means the claimant is responsible for the child. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so there is no conflict with the rule that only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. They meet all the key requirements: they live in the UK, are a UK citizen, Alex is under 16 and lives with them, they are responsible for Alex, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him.
